risk_if_not_implemented: Without USML or CCL Identification, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Foreign Ownership, Control or Influence (FOCI)
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to minimize risk associate with Foreign Ownership, Control or Influence (FOCI) through Supply Chain Risk Management (SCRM) practices.
compensating_control_1.justification: Foreign Ownership, Control or Influence (FOCI)  (TPM-12) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of USML or CCL Identification (CPL-11.1) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Process applicability. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Statutory, Regulatory & Contractual Compliance
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to facilitate the identification and implementation of relevant statutory, regulatory and contractual controls.
compensating_control_2.justification: Statutory, Regulatory & Contractual Compliance (CPL-01) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of USML or CCL Identification (CPL-11.1) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Process applicability. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
